description: This figure presents room-temperature tensile stress-strain curves comparing the mechanical behavior of low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C-based alloys. Subfigure (a) displays five quaternary Fe-20Mn-Al-C compositions (Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C, Fe-20Mn-13Al-0.75C, Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.0C, Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.0C, and Fe-20Mn-10Al-0.5C), revealing that increasing Al and C contents substantially enhances yield strength (ranging from ~550 MPa to ~900 MPa) and ultimate tensile strength (~800-1100 MPa) while generally reducing elongation. Subfigure (b) shows four quinary Fe-20Mn-Al-C-5Cr alloys (Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr, Fe-20Mn-13Al-1.3C-5Cr, Fe-20Mn-12Al-1.0C-5Cr, and Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C-5Cr), demonstrating that Cr addition improves the strength-ductility balance, with Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr achieving ~1136 MPa yield strength with ~29% elongation and Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C-5Cr exhibiting exceptional ductility (~65% elongation) at ~934 MPa ultimate tensile strength. The curves demonstrate nano-sized κ-carbide precipitation hardening effects and confirm these γ-phase alloys possess higher specific strength than conventional steels.
